ground_truth.title: Significant Cyber Incidents | Strategic Technologies Program | CSIS
ground_truth.main_content: This timeline records significant cyber incidents since 2006, focusing on cyber attacks on government agencies, defense and high tech companies, or economic crimes with losses of more than a million dollars. If you think we've missed something, please send an email to strategictech@csis.org. The timeline includes incidents through October 2025, documenting state-sponsored attacks, espionage campaigns, ransomware incidents, data breaches, and large-scale cyber operations by nation-state actors and criminal groups worldwide. Notable incidents include: North Korean Lazarus APT group's targeting of European defense companies to steal drone component information; a $1.5 billion Ethereum theft from Dubai-based exchange ByBit by North Korean hackers; the largest cryptocurrency heist to date; Chinese cyber espionage operations surging by 150% in 2024; and ongoing cyberattacks on Ukraine which increased by nearly 70% in 2024.
with: This timeline records significant cyber incidents since 2006, focusing on cyber attacks on government agencies, defense and high tech companies, or economic crimes with losses of more than a million dollars.
The ransomware group Medusa claimed responsibility for a data breach involving SimonMed Imaging, during which hackers exfiltrated data belonging to approximately 1.2 million patients.
North Korean Lazarus APT group targeted three European defense sector companies to steal sensitive information on drone components and manufacturing processes.
North Korean hackers stole $1.5 billion in Ethereum from the Dubai-based exchange ByBit, making it the largest cryptocurrency heist to date.
Cyberattacks on Taiwan by Chinese groups doubled to 2.4 million daily attempts in 2024, primarily targeting government systems and telecommunications firms.
